title: Pokemon Detail Validation, Derived Types, Units, and Inclusive Presentation · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for pokemon detail validation, derived types, units, and inclusive presentation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for validated inclusive Pokemon detail card. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Validate numeric ID or constrained canonical name and retain both requested input and resolved resource identity.
Validate ID, name, types, abilities, stats, height, weight, sprites or references, bounds, and member shapes from unknown.
Map transport fields into a readonly discriminated view that separates available, empty, and unsupported information.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Pokedex Ts Detail Identity Pokedex product engineering and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Pokedex Ts Detail Identity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript team handles case 9998 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Pokemon detail JSON, derived view types, units, ordered sections, and inclusive output; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate numeric ID or constrained canonical name and retain both requested input and resolved resource identity.
